# POMS latest analysis
## analysing the poms 1-6 batches dataset
```{python}
import scanpy as sc
import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
import scvi
import os
import celltypist
from celltypist import models
import re
import pertpy as pt
import muon as mu
import mudata as md
import sys
import torch
import pandas as pd
from plotnine import *
```

## load in just the RNA element of the data (will come back to the surface Ab data)
### checking size of object to get a rough idea of what I'm dealing with
```{python}
adata = mu.read(filename = "mudata_poms1-6_adtSoupX.h5mu/rna")
adata
(sys.getsizeof(adata) / (1024**3))
```

## appears Tim has already done some preliminary analysis, so checking the QC metrics to confirm data is filtered
## i also check counts, logcounts and X to confirm normalisation and where the raw data is
```{python}
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(12,8))

# Define a GridSpec with 6 equal-width units
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 6, height_ratios=[1,1.2], figure=fig)

# Top row: 3 violins, each spans 2 units (so all equal width)
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0:2])
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 2:4])
ax3 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 4:6])

sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_mt", ax=ax1, show=False, stripplot=False)
sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_ribo", ax=ax2, show=False, stripplot=False)
sc.pl.violin(adata, "pct_counts_hb", ax=ax3, show=False, stripplot=False)

# Bottom row: 2 histograms, each spans 3 units (1.5 of the top-row plots)
ax4 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 0:3])
ax5 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1, 3:6])

sns.histplot(np.sum(adata.layers["counts"], axis=1), bins=100, color="blue", ax=ax4)
ax4.set_title("Counts")

sns.histplot(np.sum(adata.layers["logcounts"], axis=1), bins=100, color="red", ax=ax5)
ax5.set_title("Logcounts")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

plt.savefig("POMS_QC_postfiltering.png", dpi=300)
```

## at this point i also extract all of the obs into a df so i can inspect it and check what the data has
### for example im checking how many cells belong to each patient and in which pool they belong
```{python}
obs_df = adata.obs.copy()
counts = obs_df.groupby(['poolID', 'patient_nice']).size().reset_index(name='n_cells')
counts_actual = counts[counts['n_cells'] > 0]
plt.figure()
sns.barplot(data=counts_actual, x='poolID', y='n_cells', hue='patient_nice')
```

## this showed me that there are some duplicate pools, or a situation where a patient was sequencing across two pools, therefore i will rename these to make them usable for batch correction by moving these into one combined pool
```{python}
adata.obs["pool_new"] = adata.obs["poolID"].map(
    {
    "sc12":"sc12_13",
    "sc13":"sc12_13",
    "sc14":"sc14_15",
    "sc15":"sc14_15",
    "sc16":"sc16_17",
    "sc17":"sc16_17",
    "sc18":"sc18_19",
    "sc19":"sc18_19",
    "Pool_3":"Pool_3",
    "Pool_4":"Pool_4",
    }
)
new_counts = adata.obs.groupby(['pool_new', 'patient_nice']).size().reset_index(name='n_cells')
```

## we want to analyse fetal samples separately, so pulling out and re-running processing
```{python}
pom_fetal = adata[adata.obs["fetmat"].isin(["Fetal"])].copy()
del adata
pom_fetal.obs["fetmat"].value_counts()
```

## make sure X is set to the counts and then normalise, before saving the logcounts again
```{python}
pom_fetal.X = pom_fetal.layers["counts"].copy()
sc.pp.normalize_total(pom_fetal)
sc.pp.log1p(pom_fetal)
pom_fetal.layers["logcounts"] = pom_fetal.X.copy()
```

## before batch correction, let's assess the state of the umap to see how strong the effects are by running the basic pipeline
```{python}
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(pom_fetal)
sc.tl.pca(pom_fetal)
sc.pp.neighbors(pom_fetal)
sc.tl.umap(pom_fetal)
pom_fetal
```

## huge batch effects are seen
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(20,15))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color=["pool_new"], wspace=1,show=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("UMAP_uncorrected.png",dpi=300)
```

## first corrective step is to perform batch-aware hvg selection - scanpy has this built in
```{python}
sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(
    pom_fetal, n_top_genes=2000, flavor="cell_ranger", batch_key="pool_new"
)
pom_fetal.var
```
```{python}
plt.figure()
n_batches = pom_fetal.var["highly_variable_nbatches"].value_counts()
ax = n_batches.plot(kind="bar")
n_batches
```

## data is then subetted to the hvgs for scvi to be run on - the embeddings will be added back to the full data later
```{python}
pom_hvg = pom_fetal[:, pom_fetal.var["highly_variable"]].copy()
pom_hvg
```

## set up the scvi model
```{python}
scvi.model.SCVI.setup_anndata(pom_hvg, layer="counts", batch_key="pool_new")
pom_hvg
model_scvi = scvi.model.SCVI(pom_hvg)
model_scvi
model_scvi.view_anndata_setup()
```

## automatically calculate the epoch number (eopchs = number of interations the model performs)
```{python}
max_epochs_scvi = np.min([round((20000 / pom_fetal.n_obs) * 400), 400])
max_epochs_scvi
```

## run the model
```{python}
model_scvi.train()
```

## extract the embedding and add it to the full data
## calculate batch-correcting UMAP
```{python}
pom_fetal.obsm["X_scVI"] = model_scvi.get_latent_representation()
sc.pp.neighbors(pom_fetal, use_rep="X_scVI")
sc.tl.umap(pom_fetal)
```

```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(20,15))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color=["pool_new"], wspace=1,show=True)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("UMAP_scvi.png",dpi=300)
```

## save/reload object here
```{python}
pom_fetal.write(filename="POMS_fetal_batch1-6.h5ad")
```

## before clustering, will run celltypist to get a rough idea of how many clusters to expect, as this will guide my selection of resolution in the next steps
## as good practice, i remove any existing metadata fields related to celltypist (especially elevant here as it appears tim might have run it before) as this will block a new prediction
```{python}
models.download_models(force_update = True)
model_low = models.Model.load(model="Immune_All_Low.pkl")
model_high = models.Model.load(model="Immune_All_High.pkl")
model_fetal = models.Model.load(model="Pan_Fetal_Human.pkl")


keywords = ["celltypist", "predicted_labels", "majority_voting", "CT"]
cols_to_remove = [c for c in pom_fetal.obs.columns if any(kw in c.lower() for kw in keywords)]
pom_fetal.obs.drop(columns=cols_to_remove, inplace=True)

models_and_cols = [
    ("Immune_All_Low.pkl", "CT_low"),
    ("Immune_All_High.pkl", "CT_high"),
    ("Pan_Fetal_Human.pkl", "CT_fetal")
]

for model_file, col_name in models_and_cols:
    # Annotate and get results
    res = celltypist.annotate(pom_fetal, model=model_file, majority_voting=True)
    
    # Extract the predicted_labels DataFrame
    df_labels = res.predicted_labels  # this should be a pandas DataFrame
    
    # If majority_voting is present, you may choose to use it
    if "majority_voting" in df_labels.columns:
        sel = df_labels["majority_voting"]
    else:
        sel = df_labels["predicted_labels"]
    
    # Align and assign to pom_b.obs
    pom_fetal.obs[col_name] = sel.reindex(pom_fetal.obs_names).values

    # Extract confidence for each predicted label
    # Confidence columns are all except 'predicted_labels' and 'majority_voting'
    confidence_cols = [c for c in df_labels.columns if c not in ["predicted_labels", "majority_voting"]]
    
    # Map each cell's predicted label to its confidence
    confidences = df_labels.apply(
        lambda row: row[row["majority_voting"] if "majority_voting" in row else row["predicted_labels"]],
        axis=1
    )
    
    # Store confidence in obs
    pom_fetal.obs[col_name + "_conf"] = confidences.reindex(pom_fetal.obs_names).values
    
    # Optional: print summary
    print(f"{col_name} label counts:")
    print(predicted.value_counts().head())
    print(f"{col_name} confidence stats:")
    print(confidences.describe())

plt.figure(figsize=(3,20))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color=["CT_high", "CT_low", "CT_fetal"], ncols=1, palette="tab20")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("celltypist_predictions.png",dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

plt.figure(figsize=(3,20))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color=[""], ncols=1, palette="tab20")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("celltypist_predictions.png",dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
```

## run leiden clustering
```{python}
 for res in [0.25,0.3,0.4,0.5,0.6,0.7,0.8,0.9,1,1.1,1.2,1.3,1.4,1.5,1.6,1.7,1.8,1.9,2]:
    sc.tl.leiden(pom_fetal, key_added = "leiden_res_" + str(res), resolution = res)
```

## view various resolutions
```{python}
plt.figure(figsize=(5,10))
sc.pl.umap(
    pom_fetal,
    color=["leiden_res_1.1", "leiden_res_1.2", "leiden_res_1.3",
     "leiden_res_1.4", "leiden_res_1.5", "leiden_res_1.6", "leiden_res_1.7",
     "leiden_res_1.8", "leiden_res_1.9", "leiden_res_2"], 
    size = 50, legend_fontsize="x-large", ncols=5
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("clustering_resolutions.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
```

## plot some key marker genes to get an idea of what celltypes lie where
```{python}
key_genes = ["PRDM1", "CD3D", "CD4", "CD8A", "CCR7", "SELL", "FOXP3", "CTLA4", "NKG7", "GZMB", "GZMK", "NCAM1", "CD19", "IGHD", "IGHM", "TCL1A", "CR2", "CD24", "CD27", "CD38", "MME", "IL10", "CD5", "SPN", "LYZ", "CD14", "FCGR3A", "CD1C", "IRF7", "CD34", "TRDV2", "KLRB1", "MKI67", "PPBP", "HBB"]
plt.figure(figsize=(20,24))
sc.pl.umap(
    pom_fetal, ncols=7,
    color=key_genes, sort_order=False, vmax="p99",
    size = 30, layer = "logcounts", cmap="magma"
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("UMAP_key_markers.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
```

## after assessment of umaps and markers i am choosing resolution of 1.6 to take forward for annotation 
```{python}
plt.figure()
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color="leiden_res_1.6", size=30, legend_loc="on data")
plt.savefig("UMAP_res_1.6.png", dpi=300)
sc.pl.dotplot(pom_fetal, groupby="leiden_res_1.6", var_names=key_genes, standard_scale="var")
plt.figure()
sc.pl.stacked_violin(pom_fetal, var_names=key_genes, groupby = "leiden_res_1.6",
                     use_raw=False, layer="logcounts", stripplot = False)
plt.savefig("violin_key_markers.png", dpi=300)
```

## calculate DEGs for each cluster at the 1.6 resolution and plot them
```{python}
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(
    pom_fetal, groupby="leiden_res_1.6", method="wilcoxon", key_added="dea_res_1.6"
)
```

```{python}
plt.figure()
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(
    pom_fetal, groupby="leiden_res_1.6", standard_scale="var", n_genes=5, key="dea_res_1.6"
)
plt.savefig("dotplot_degs_res_1.6.png", dpi=300)
```

## given the complexity of the t cells, I'll make a separate violin plot focusing on T cell markers
```{python}
t_genes = ["CD3D", "CD4", "CD8A", "CCR7", "SELL", "CD27", "CD28", "IL7R", "TBX21", "GATA3", "RORC", "FOXP3", "CTLA4", "GZMB", "GZMK", "CCR4", "CCR6", "CXCR3", "CD69", "ITGAE", "FAS", "IL2RA"]
plt.figure()
sc.pl.stacked_violin(pom_fetal, var_names=t_genes, groupby = "leiden_res_1.6",
                     use_raw=False, layer="logcounts", stripplot = False)
plt.savefig("violin_T_cell_markers.png", dpi=300)
```

## also running the dea on suspected B cell clusters to see whats different about them
```{python}
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(
    pom_fetal, groupby="leiden_res_1.6", groups=["0", "6", "17"], reference= "0", method="wilcoxon", key_added="dea_res_1.6_b_clusters"
)
sc.pl.rank_genes_groups_dotplot(
    pom_fetal, groupby="leiden_res_1.6", standard_scale="var", n_genes=5, key="dea_res_1.6_b_clusters"
)
```

## build the annotation dictionary (you will keep coming back here to tweak things!)
```{python}
pom_fetal.obs["labels"] = pom_fetal.obs["leiden_res_1.6"].map(
    {
        "0": "B_naive",
        "1": "CD8+T_naive",
        "2": "CD4+T_naive",
        "3": "CD4+T_naive",
        "4": "CD8+T_naive",
        "5": "CD4+T_naive",
        "6": "B_naive",
        "7": "CD4+T_naive",
        "8": "CD4+T_naive",
        "9": "CD4+T_naive",
        "10": "Monocyte",
        "11": "NK",
        "12": "Treg",
        "13": "CD4+T_naive",
        "14": "CD4+T_naive",
        "15": "CD4+T_naive",
        "16": "ILC_CCR6+",
        "17": "B_B1",
        "18": "CD8+T_memory/ILC",
        "19": "ILC",
        "20": "HSC",
        "21": "CD4+T_naive",
        "22": "DC",
        "23": "RBC",
        "24": "Platelet",
    }
)
```

## annotated umap
```{python}
cluster_palette = ["limegreen", "darkgreen", "cornflowerblue", "darkred", "indianred", "goldenrod", "black", "darkorchid", "slateblue", "orange", "deeppink", "darkgrey", "slategrey","blue"]
plt.figure(figsize=(10,10))
sc.pl.umap(pom_fetal, color="labels", size=30, legend_loc="on data", frameon=False,show=False,
palette=cluster_palette, legend_fontoutline=2, legend_fontsize= "small", title="Annotated UMAP")
plt.savefig("UMAP_annoated.png", dpi=300)
plt.show()

label_genes = ["CD3D", "CD4", "CD8A", "CCR7", "SELL", "FOXP3", "CTLA4", "KLRB1", "CCR6", "NCAM1", "NKG7", "GZMB", "GZMK", "CD19", "IGHD", "IGHM", "TCL1A", "CR2", "CD5", "LYZ", "CD14", "FCGR3A", "CD1C", "IRF7", "TRDV2", "CD34", "PPBP", "HBB"]

plt.figure(figsize=(5,8))
sc.pl.stacked_violin(pom_fetal, var_names=label_genes, groupby = "labels",
                     use_raw=False, layer="logcounts", stripplot = False,
                     row_palette=cluster_palette,show=False)
plt.savefig("violin_annotated.png", dpi=300)
plt.show()
```

## save/reload object here
```{python}
pom_fetal.write(filename="POMS_fetal_batch1-6_annoated.h5ad")
```

## assess cluster proportions over time
## first we'll use gestaional week and perform linear regressions and aftwerwards we'll bin them into categories
## adding a categorical, binned gestation column for further comparisons
```{python}
pom_fetal.obs["gestation_binned"] = pom_fetal.obs["gestation_week"].map(
    {
        23.4: "<25_pgw",
        23.6: "<25_pgw",
        23.7: "<25_pgw",
        24.1: "<25_pgw",
        24.4: "<25_pgw",
        25.0: "<28_pgw",
        25.3: "<28_pgw",
        25.4: "<28_pgw",
        26.9: "<28_pgw",
        27.3: "<28_pgw",
        29.6: ">28_pgw",
        32.7: ">28_pgw",
        33.1: ">28_pgw",
        35.9: ">28_pgw"
    }
)
```

## visualising UMAPs over time to support these findings
```{python}
plt.figure()
for bin_name in pom_fetal.obs['gestation_binned'].unique():
    sc.pl.umap(
        pom_fetal[pom_fetal.obs['gestation_binned'] == bin_name, :],
        color='labels',
        size=30, show=False,
        palette=cluster_palette,
        title=f"UMAP {bin_name}"
    )
plt.savefig("UMAP_by_gestation.png", dpi=300)
plt.show()
```

## pull out data into a df
```{python}
cluster_key = "labels"
sample_key = "patient_nice"

counts = (
    pom_fetal.obs
        .groupby([sample_key, cluster_key])
        .size()
        .unstack(fill_value=0)
)

proportions = (counts.div(counts.sum(axis=1), axis=0)*100)

patient_meta_cols = ["gestation_week", "gestation_binned"]

patient_meta = (
    pom_fetal.obs[[sample_key] + patient_meta_cols]
    .drop_duplicates(subset=sample_key)
    .set_index(sample_key)
)

proportions_df = pd.concat([patient_meta,proportions], axis=1)

proportions_df.index.name = "sample_id"
proportions_df = proportions_df.reset_index()
proportions_df = pd.melt(proportions_df, id_vars=["sample_id","gestation_week", "gestation_binned"], value_vars=proportions_df.columns[3:17], var_name="cluster", value_name="percentage")

```

```{python}
plt.figure()
reg_plot = sns.lmplot(
    data=proportions_df,
    x="gestation_week",
    y="percentage",
    col="cluster",
    col_wrap=4,
    hue="cluster",
    palette=cluster_palette,
    height=5,
    aspect=1.5,
    facet_kws=dict(sharey=False),
    ci=None,
    scatter_kws={'s': 100},
    line_kws={'linewidth': 5}
    
)

reg_plot.set_titles(col_template="{col_name}", size=24)
reg_plot.set_xlabels(fontsize=24)
reg_plot.set_ylabels(fontsize=24)
reg_plot.savefig("proportions_by_gestation.png", dpi=300)  
plt.show()
```


## boxplots
## note here i have started using the plotnine package, which utilises ggplot from R (which is the GOAT of data visualisation)
## i'll go back at some point and update other plots
```{python}
plt.figure()
sns.catplot(data=proportions_df,x="gestation_binned",y="percentage",kind="box", hue="gestation_binned", palette="vlag",col="cluster", col_wrap=4, facet_kws=dict(sharey=False))
plt.savefig("proportions_binned.png", dpi=300)
plt.show()

```
